key_points: No AC in dorms is a real complaint, not just a rumor
First-year singles are rare and hard to request successfully
Residential college identity is strong; nicknames like 'Morsels' and 'Spiders' are real
key_quotes: No AC in dorms, traveling to airport and back home for break is expensive
I'm quite anxious/averse to having a roommate... the single thing has always been the top priority
Some res colleges have affectionate nicknames. Students in Morse called each other Morsels.

key_points: Social scene is consistently rated better than Harvard's by students
Extracurricular clubs are highly competitive with auditions and full applications required
Small campus means everyone knows your business, including breakups
key_quotes: Yes, Yale's social life is significantly better than Harvard's by a pretty big margin. Parties are every weekend.
Everything is so ungodly competitive. Getting into seminar classes requires suck up paragraphs and sometimes full applications.
If you date someone, everyone in ur grade will know, and if you two break up, everyone will want to know why.

key_points: Dining halls inaccessible to grad students without awkward workarounds
Yale cut free Adobe Creative Cloud with almost no notice, suggesting more cuts possible
At least one beloved dining item (pear cardamom olive oil cake) has cult following
key_quotes: The PhD students told me I can just walk in and pay with a credit card, but you can't access the dining hall unless you are an undergrad.
I think they removed HBOmax as well. I can't seem to access it through xfinity.
I am 1000% certain at some point someone had access to the Yale Dining pear cardamom olive oil cake recipe and I NEED it.

key_points: Imposter syndrome is widespread, especially among FGLI students
Freshman year loneliness and bullying by suitemates are documented experiences
Pressure is internal and self-competitive, not student-vs-student, but still intense
key_quotes: I struggled severely with my mental health, got bullied by my suitemates, and I don't really have any friends outside of the faces I've spoken to a few times.
You're gonna feel out of place a lot. Just about all of us did.
Students at Yale tend to be very utilitarian and make things way more exclusive than they need to be.

key_points: Yale is need-blind for internationals and meets 100% of demonstrated need — genuinely
Outside scholarships reduce Yale aid dollar-for-dollar, a frustrating trap for scholarship winners
Students on full aid are anxious about budget cuts affecting work-study and RA positions
key_quotes: I have around $20,000 in outside scholarships, and my parents and I are slightly worried that it is all hurting me more than it is helping.
After weeks and weeks of talking to my financial aid officer, I had to accept the truth that there wasn't anything that can be done.
I'm a bit anxious about what this might mean... I was hoping for a RA/TF position, or work studies, but now I'm unsure how available those will be.

key_points: STEM is stronger than its reputation suggests; faculty teaching quality is praised
Most professors are researchers first; finding a real 'teacher' is rare and lucky
Directed Studies is intense; a cappella, theater, and DS simultaneously is a burnout risk
key_quotes: It's really rare to find a real teacher on the staff. Most of the professors are researchers, more so in the humanities areas.
Yale has absolutely phenomenal teachers — hard to compare to other places but I can say that.
Being around such ridiculously talented people all the time changes you for the better but it's also a lot of pressure.

key_points: Yale Law admissions officer reportedly threatened to identify Reddit complainers
MSN nursing program described as disorganized, exploitative, and riding on reputation
Yale cut Adobe Creative Cloud with almost no transition notice to students
key_quotes: The speaker made several negative comments along the lines of 'don't get on reddit and complain about us because if you have an email in our system we will figure out who you are.'
Administration is fine with failing a large chunk of the cohort and forcing them to take a leave of absence.
It also seems like they've given next to no notice for the transition, since we have to purchase licenses starting in August.

key_points: New Haven crime is real but often exaggerated; nighttime still warrants caution
Campus itself is stunning — described as 'Hogwarts' — but surrounded by stark poverty
Yale's walled-off fortress design isolates students from the city it largely ignores
key_quotes: It's like stepping into Hogwarts after passing by a brigade of homeless and tweakers.
Yale rivals only Columbia in terms of being a walled-off fortress to protect its students from the dangerous surrounding community that it has neglected for nearly a century.
Town-gown relations have long been poor, and one consequence is that the area simply is not safe. I'd be very careful going out at night.

key_points: Yale is #2 per capita feeder to top Wall Street firms, likely #1 adjusted for firm prestige
Elite pipeline placement (banking, consulting, law, medicine) is consistently strong
STEM grads increasingly worry Yale lags MIT/Stanford for tech industry placement
key_quotes: Yale is the #2 per capita feeder to top firms (GS, JPM, MS, etc.) based on LinkedIn data, after Columbia which is based right in NYC.
Being seen as the 'humanities' school among HYPSM doesn't make it very sexy in the increasingly tech-dominated world.
Based on the sheer number of Yale grads going into consulting, banking, and finance, this makes sense.

key_points: If you get strong financial aid, Yale is an exceptional value — essentially unbeatable
At full pay (~$85k/year), students debate whether prestige premium over WashU is worth $20k+
Outside scholarship holders get punished; Yale claws back aid dollar-for-dollar
key_quotes: It is. A Yale degree will open doors for you throughout your career that a WashU degree can't.
My family could pay for Yale, but not without flinching. They wouldn't have to change lifestyles, but it wouldn't exactly be pocket change.
Why should Yale or any other peer college ignore outside scholarship funds that reduce your financial need?

key_points: Students describe Yale as collaborative and less cutthroat than Princeton or Harvard
Community is tight-knit, sometimes claustrophobically so — everyone knows everyone
Strong institutional pride coexists with honest awareness of flaws like New Haven and STEM gaps
key_quotes: Less grindy and individually competitive than Princeton.
I chose Yale over Harvard and I don't regret it for a second. Yale students were happier and more well-rounded.
You will be surrounded by the best and most interesting people you've ever met.

key_points: **Administration:** Law school AO explicitly threatened to identify Reddit critics
**Academics:** MSN nursing program described as exploitative and failing students deliberately
**Financial:** Outside scholarships reduce aid dollar-for-dollar with no workaround
key_quotes: 'Don't get on reddit and complain about us because if you have an email in our system we will figure out who you are.'
I wish someone had told me honestly before I paid tuition here that I should not come here. The MSN program is not good, it is a waste of money.
After weeks of talking to my financial aid officer, I had to accept the truth that there wasn't anything that can be done.

key_points: Underground tunnels connect buildings with basketball courts, game rooms, and kitchens
Residential college system creates genuinely close communities with unique identities
Applied math placement into quant finance is among the best in the country
key_quotes: Yale has awesome basements! They connect everywhere on campus and had basketball courts, kitchens, dining rooms, game rooms.
If by finance/engineering you mean quant, then Yale is probably among the top 5 schools in terms of placement of students in quant recruiting.
The Blue Book was an actual hard copy. I loved getting it every year and perusing all the amazing course offerings — so many I never got to take.
